justification: All rubric criteria met: both training (weekly batch) and serving (real-time prediction service) pipelines are fully designed with clear components and data flows. The 100ms P99 constraint is explicitly addressed with latency budget allocation (feature fetch ≤10ms, inference ≤20ms, overhead ≤10ms, buffer ≤60ms) and model complexity guidance (gradient-boosted trees over deep learning). Feature store is identified as necessary with dual-layer design (online Redis + offline Parquet). Model versioning via immutable Model Registry with blue/green deployment and rollback mechanism. Team size factored throughout — recommends FastAPI/Prefect over heavier alternatives, human-in-the-loop promotion, and explicitly warns against per-zone models for 4 people. Trade-offs and risks are comprehensive and well-reasoned.

observations.text: Excellent separation of hot path (serving) and cold path (training) with clear latency budget breakdown for the 100ms P99 constraint
Feature store dual-layer design (online/offline) with emphasis on training/serving feature parity as the #1 risk
Blue/green model deployment with instant rollback via pointer swap — simple and appropriate for team size
Model Registry with immutable versioning enables safe rollback to version N-1
Technology recommendations consistently calibrated for a 4-person team (Prefect over Airflow, human-in-the-loop before auto-promotion)
Shadow scoring pattern identified — comparing new model to active model before promotion
Phased implementation plan (3 phases over 6 weeks) is realistic and well-sequenced
Identifies feature staleness risk and model drift risk explicitly
ONNX Runtime recommendation decouples model format from training framework — good flexibility point

Does not explicitly recommend managed services (SageMaker, Vertex AI) as the rubric expects for a 4-person team — instead recommends self-managed but lightweight components (FastAPI, Prefect, MLflow). The choices are pragmatic but the managed-service option is only implicit.

agent_output_excerpt:
## Executive Summary

This is a **real-time ML inference system** with a weekly retraining loop. The architecture separates the hot path (sub-100ms prediction serving) from the cold path (weekly training pipeline), connected by a model registry that enables safe rollbacks. Given a 4-person team, I'm designing for operational simplicity — pre-computed feature stores, a lightweight model server behind the existing API, and blue/green model deployment rather than complex canary infrastructure.

---
